meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing & Version Control for Students | AI Builder Guide
content.seo_description: Stop losing code and start monetizing. Learn how to implement SaaS pricing and version control using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert guide.
content.answer_block: Version control is the lifeblood of software engineering; without it, you are building on quicksand. By integrating Git-based versioning with a robust SaaS pricing model, you secure your intellectual property while creating a sustainable revenue loop. Use Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code to automate your deployment pipeline.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks coding your first SaaS, only to realize that one accidental 'rm -rf' or a bad merge could vaporize your entire repository. The absence of version control is the silent killer of student projects, turning potential portfolio pieces into digital ghosts. It is time to treat your code like the asset it is, rather than a temporary experiment.

Simultaneously, the bridge between a 'project' and a 'business' is pricing. When you implement a structured pricing model, you force yourself to understand the value your software delivers. We are moving beyond simple coding; we are engineering a professional ecosystem where your logic is preserved by versioning and your value is validated by a transaction layer.

steps.title: Initialize Repository Architecture
steps.description: Before writing a single line of feature code, you must establish a secure Git foundation. This prevents the 'no version control' nightmare and enables iterative development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a professional production-grade project structure with .gitignore, README.md, and a standard Git flow configuration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude git init && claude add . && claude commit -m 'Initial project structure'

steps.title: Define SaaS Pricing Logic
steps.description: Inject a modular pricing engine into your codebase. You need a structure that handles tiers, subscription intervals, and feature flags.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular pricing model schema that supports Basic, Pro, and Enterprise tiers with boolean feature flags.

steps.title: Implement Version-Controlled Webhooks
steps.description: Connect your pricing logic to a payment gateway, ensuring all integration code is tracked within your repository.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure Stripe webhook listener that validates subscription status against my pricing model flags.

steps.title: Automate Deployment Pipelines
steps.description: Use Claude Code to automate the deployment process, ensuring every push to main is tested and versioned correctly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a CI/CD pipeline that runs my test suite before deploying to production environments.

steps.title: Finalize Versioned Documentation
steps.description: Document your pricing logic so that your future self—or potential employers—understand your architectural decisions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate technical documentation for the pricing engine and version control strategy, saved in the /docs folder.

pro_tips: Always commit your pricing logic changes in a separate branch from your UI changes to keep your git history clean.
Use semantic versioning (SemVer) for your SaaS releases to signal stability to your users.
Never hardcode your pricing tiers; store them in a configuration file or database that can be updated without redeploying code.
Implement a 'feature flag' system so you can toggle pricing tiers for beta testers without breaking the main app.
Review your git diffs before every commit; it is the best way to catch bugs before they reach production.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live and your version control is active, deploy to a staging environment and run a smoke test to ensure your payment webhooks correctly toggle feature access.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are an AI Builder with a production-ready mindset. Take the leap, secure your code, and monetize your innovation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
